name: Sweet and Sour Pork
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim fat from pork. Cut pork into 3/4-inch pieces.
2. Mix together egg, cornstarch, oil, salt, light soy sauce, and white pepper in a medium bowl. Stir in pork. Cover and refrigerate for 20 minutes.
3. Cut each tomato into 8 wedges. Cut bell pepper into 1-inch pieces.
4. Heat oil (1 1/2 inches) in a wok to 350°F.
5. Mix together flour, water, cornstarch, salt, and baking soda in a medium bowl. Stir pork pieces into batter until well coated.
6. Fry about 15 pieces at a time for 4 minutes or until light brown, turning frequently. Drain on paper towels.
7. Increase oil temperature to 375°F. Fry pork all at once for 1 minute or until golden brown. Drain on paper towels.
8. Place pork on a heated platter.
9. Heat sugar, broth, vinegar, oil, dark soy sauce, salt, and garlic in a 3-quart saucepan over high heat until boiling.
10. Mix together 1/4 cup cornstarch and 1/4 cup cold water. Stir into the sauce. Cook and stir for about 20 seconds or until thickened.
11. Stir in tomatoes, bell pepper, and pineapple. Heat to boiling and pour over pork.
